channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Iran-US War News Live Updates: US Issues Worldwide Security Alert Amid Rising Middle East Tensions

media:content.media:description: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has largely stalled

item.description: US-Iran War News Live Updates: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War News Live Updates: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Firefighters Rescue Dog Missing For Weeks, Found Swimming In San Francisco Bay

media:content.media:description: The owner said she had visited the SPCA in San Mateo to find her missing pet.

item.description: The Menlo Park Fire Protection District received a call on July 13 stating that a dog had entered a body of water and was swimming.
item.content:encoded: The Menlo Park Fire Protection District received a call on July 13 stating that a dog had entered a body of water and was swimming.

item.title: FBI Issues 'Wanted' Notice Against Indian National In Drug Trafficking Case

media:content.media:description: The FBI is on the lookout for Indian national Harmanveer Singh for his alleged role in drug trafficking

item.description: The crackdown has already netted 24 arrests and 37 indictments across the US, Canada and Europe for smuggling cocaine and methamphetamine.
item.content:encoded: The crackdown has already netted 24 arrests and 37 indictments across the US, Canada and Europe for smuggling cocaine and methamphetamine.

item.title: New York Artist Spends Rs 96 Lakh On Charlie Kirk Statue, Says No One Wants To Buy It: "People Are Cheap"

media:content.media:description: His latest plan is to unveil the 6-foot-4-inch statue in New York's Times Square on September 10.

item.description: Charlie Kirk, the founder of Turning Point USA, was a prominent figure in conservative political circles before he was shot dead in September last year.
item.content:encoded: Charlie Kirk, the founder of Turning Point USA, was a prominent figure in conservative political circles before he was shot dead in September last year.

item.title: Indian Man Jailed For Attempted Theft Of Diamond Worth $1,54,000 In Singapore

media:content.media:description: Indian national jailed after diamond swap heist plot at Singapore jewellery store

item.description: Indian national Mangroliya Manojkumar Kurjibhai jailed in Singapore after swapping a genuine diamond worth USD154,000 with a fake at a Chinatown store
item.content:encoded: Indian national Mangroliya Manojkumar Kurjibhai jailed in Singapore after swapping a genuine diamond worth USD154,000 with a fake at a Chinatown store

item.title: US-Iran War Highlights: US Conducts Strikes On Iran For 9th Straight Night After Another Soldier Killed

media:content.media:description: US-Iran War News LIVE: The US-Iran tensions have escalated in recent days

item.description: US-Iran War News Highlights: CENTCOM said the raids were designed to "swiftly punish Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces who launched attacks against American service members in Jordan last night."
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War News Highlights: CENTCOM said the raids were designed to "swiftly punish Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces who launched attacks against American service members in Jordan last night."

item.title: 27 Dead, 83 Missing As Ferry Overturns Off Guyana Coast

media:content.media:description: The search for survivors is ongoing after the MV Barima flipped over late Saturday.

item.description: At least 27 people are dead and 83 more are missing after a ferry capsized off the coast of Guyana over the weekend, Prime Minister Mark Phillips said Monday.
item.content:encoded: At least 27 people are dead and 83 more are missing after a ferry capsized off the coast of Guyana over the weekend, Prime Minister Mark Phillips said Monday.

item.title: Wes Streeting Named New UK Defence Minister

media:content.media:description: UK's new PM Andy Burnham named ex-health minister Wes Streeting as his defence secretary late Monday.

item.description: Britain's new Prime Minister Andy Burnham named ex-health minister Wes Streeting as his defence secretary late Monday, the government said, as ministerial appointments ran into the night.
item.content:encoded: Britain's new Prime Minister Andy Burnham named ex-health minister Wes Streeting as his defence secretary late Monday, the government said, as ministerial appointments ran into the night.

item.title: 'Engaged Weeks Ago, Wanted New Job': Kerala Sailor Killed In Russian Attack

media:content.media:description: Joyan was set to marry in next few months.

item.description: Joyan's family got to know about his death through an email received on Monday evening from the shipping company he worked with.
item.content:encoded: Joyan's family got to know about his death through an email received on Monday evening from the shipping company he worked with.

item.title: 7 killed, Several Injured As Bus Bursts Into Flames In Vietnam

media:content.media:description: The bus was traveling from Lam Dong province to the nearby Ho Chi Minh City when at caught fire

item.description: The bus was traveling from Lam Dong province to the nearby business hub Ho Chi Minh City when at caught fire.
item.content:encoded: The bus was traveling from Lam Dong province to the nearby business hub Ho Chi Minh City when at caught fire.

item.title: Son Of Brazil's Ex-President Bolsonaro Secures US Green Card

media:content.media:description: Eduardo Bolsonaro on Monday said he received permanent US residency.

item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro said he moved to protect himself from what he called unjust persecution by Brazil's Supreme Court, not help his father.
item.content:encoded: Eduardo Bolsonaro said he moved to protect himself from what he called unjust persecution by Brazil's Supreme Court, not help his father.

item.title: "Discriminatory Treatment": US Imposes New 50% Tariffs On Certain Canadian Goods

media:content.media:description: US President Donald Trump signed orders Monday to impose new 50-percent tariffs on some Canadian goods.

item.description: US President Donald Trump signed orders Monday to impose new 50-percent tariffs on some Canadian goods, citing "discriminatory treatment" in the areas of alcohol, automobiles and dairy products.
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump signed orders Monday to impose new 50-percent tariffs on some Canadian goods, citing "discriminatory treatment" in the areas of alcohol, automobiles and dairy products.

item.title: France Says Two Of Its Diplomats "Detained, Physically Abused" In Iran

media:content.media:description: French Foreign Minister said that the two diplomats were targeted by the Iranian officials late Sunday.

item.description: Two French diplomats in Iran suffered "an extremely serious act of intimidation" by Islamic republic security services, with one of them physically abused, France's foreign minister said Monday.
item.content:encoded: Two French diplomats in Iran suffered "an extremely serious act of intimidation" by Islamic republic security services, with one of them physically abused, France's foreign minister said Monday.

item.title: Jordan Says It Downed 3 Iranian Missiles Targeting Kingdom

media:content.media:description: Jordan said that the air defence system intercepted and downed tonight three missiles coming from Iran.

item.description: Jordan's army said on Monday that air defences had downed three Iranian missiles targeting the kingdom, with no injuries or damage reported.
item.content:encoded: Jordan's army said on Monday that air defences had downed three Iranian missiles targeting the kingdom, with no injuries or damage reported.

item.title: Trump Discusses Trade, Military Alliance, Hormuz With New UK PM

media:content.media:description: Trump said he had a "very good conversation" with Burnham.

item.description: US President Donald Trump said on Monday he had a "very good conversation" with new British Prime Minister Andy Burnham, adding he would be meeting him "in the not too distant future."
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump said on Monday he had a "very good conversation" with new British Prime Minister Andy Burnham, adding he would be meeting him "in the not too distant future."

item.title: US Slams "Dangerous" China Action In Sea Clash With Philippines Navy

media:content.media:description: The Philippine military has accused the China Coast Guard of clubbing a Filipino sailor with a baton.

item.description: Washington on Monday condemned what it called "dangerous and aggressive" action by China, whose personnel are accused of injuring a Filipino sailor at a shoal in the disputed South China Sea.
item.content:encoded: Washington on Monday condemned what it called "dangerous and aggressive" action by China, whose personnel are accused of injuring a Filipino sailor at a shoal in the disputed South China Sea.

item.title: Missiles Hit US Base In Kuwait As Iran Declares "Full-Scale War"

media:content.media:description: Iran said that its army's ground forces targeted the HIMARS missile systems of the US army in Kuwait.

item.description: The Iranian army said early Tuesday it had targeted a US base in Kuwait with missiles, as President Masoud Pezeshkian said the conflict had morphed into a "full-scale war".
item.content:encoded: The Iranian army said early Tuesday it had targeted a US base in Kuwait with missiles, as President Masoud Pezeshkian said the conflict had morphed into a "full-scale war".

item.title: US Launches New Round Of Strikes On Iran, Now 10th Night In A Row

media:content.media:description: US said othat the latest strikes "are designed to further degrade Iranian military capabilities".

item.description: The United States military said late Monday it was carrying out its 10th consecutive night of strikes on Iran in a push to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1130585/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>The United States military said late Monday it was carrying out its 10th consecutive night of strikes on Iran in a push to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Israel's Leading Leftist Party Launches Electoral Campaign

media:content.media:description: Israel's largest leftist party on Monday launched its campaign for the legislative election.

item.description: Israel's largest leftist party on Monday launched its campaign for the legislative election due to be held in October, after having selected its list of candidates in the primaries.
item.content:encoded: Israel's largest leftist party on Monday launched its campaign for the legislative election due to be held in October, after having selected its list of candidates in the primaries.

item.title: 20 Killed, 100 Missing In Afghanistan Flooding

media:content.media:description: Twenty bodies have been recovered so far, while over 80 were injured.

item.description: Only mud remained where riverside shops previously stood in Parun, the provincial capital where residents were searching for their mayor among others.
item.content:encoded: Only mud remained where riverside shops previously stood in Parun, the provincial capital where residents were searching for their mayor among others.
